page: Microsoft Azure D8ads_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine based on the x86_64 architecture, powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with 2 threads per core, operating at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. The CPU cache configuration includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

This server includes 307 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, allowing for high-performance disk attachments. Virtualization is managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The CPU supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX, AES, VAES, SHA_NI, BMI2, ADX, FMA, and AMD SVM virtualization, though it lacks AVX-512 extensions.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core CPU performance, placing in the top 25% of tested systems. Multi-core CPU performance, Geekbench scores, and Passmark CPU metrics fall within the average range. Memory read bandwidth is average, reaching 514.81 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 39.11 GB/sec for uncached large blocks, though large block write bandwidth is relatively weak at 22.63 GB/sec. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression also registers in the bottom 25%.

Given its balanced vCPU-to-RAM ratio and local storage capacity, the D8ads_v5 is well-suited for general-purpose workloads, static web serving, software compilation, and moderate database operations. It offers a balanced resource profile, making it a cost-efficient option for workloads requiring dedicated AMD EPYC compute resources and local SSD storage without the premium cost of memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated instances.
description: Microsoft Azure D8ads_v5 is an x86_64 general-purpose virtual machine powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs, 32.0 GB of RAM, and 307 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability. Operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, the instance delivers strong single-core CPU performance, while its multi-core, memory read bandwidth, and database operations benchmarks align with average mid-range performance. It exhibits weaker performance in large-block memory writes and multi-threaded decompression. The balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and integrated local storage make this server a cost-efficient choice for general-purpose applications, static web serving, software compilation, and moderate database workloads that do not require GPU acceleration.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D8ads_v5 is an 8-vCPU general-purpose virtual machine featuring an AMD EPYC 7763 processor, 32 GB RAM, 307 GB of local storage, and strong single-core performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D8ads_v5 is an 8-vCPU general-purpose virtual machine featuring an AMD EPYC 7763 processor, 32 GB RAM, and 307 GB of local SSD storage.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC compute, integrated local storage, and strong single-core processing capabilities.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 32 GB RAM.
Includes 307 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability.
Delivers strong single-core CPU performance in benchmark testing.
Features a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio for general-purpose workloads.
Suitable for static web serving, software compilation, and database operations.
